std::vector< double > quadWeights
 Output weights (include Jacobians)
 

Detailed Description

Polar \((\rho, \theta)\) quadrature generator on a triangular facet.

Workflow:

  • Construct with a triangle, an observation point, and a 1D Gauss rule (nodes/weights) of size nQuadrature for both \(\rho\) and \(\theta\) expansions.
  • The constructor calls adaptPolar() which:
    • Projects geometry to the triangle plane (transformInPlane()).
    • Determines angular sectors and their radial bounds (adaptIn() if the point lies inside the facet's projection, else adaptOut()).
    • Assembles barycentric quadrature points/weights (assembleFromPolar()).
  • Retrieve integration nodes/weights via getQuadPoints()/getQuadWeights().

Definition at line 36 of file TriQuadPol.h.

Constructor & Destructor Documentation

◆ TriQuadPol()

TriQuadPol::TriQuadPol ( Triangle * inTp,
rvec inPos,
int nQuadrature,
double(*) xQuadrature[2],
double * wQuadrature )

Construct and immediately adapt the polar quadrature.

Parameters
inTpTriangle pointer.
inPosObservation point in 3D.
nQuadratureNumber of nodes for the 1D Gauss rule ( \(\rho\) and \(\theta\)).
xQuadratureArray of Gauss nodes in [0,1] (size nQuadrature x 2; uses [i][0]).
wQuadratureArray of corresponding Gauss weights (size nQuadrature).

Stores the inputs and calls adaptPolar() to generate nodes/weights.

Member Function Documentation

◆ adaptIn()

void TriQuadPol::adaptIn ( double theta0,
double thetaStart,
double thetaEnd,
double tLine,
double dLine,
std::vector< double > & intThetas,
std::vector< double > & intWeights,
std::vector< double > & minRadius,
std::vector< double > & maxRadius )

Build angular bins and radial bounds when the point is inside the triangle.

Uses a single boundary line per angular sector.

Parameters
theta0Global rotation added to all output angles.
thetaStart,thetaEndSector angular limits.
tLine,dLineLine parameters from lineInPolar() \(\{\theta_0, d_0\}\).
intThetas,intWeightsOutput Gauss nodes/weights in \(\theta\).
minRadius,maxRadiusOutput inner/outer radii for each \(\theta\) bin.

Build a θ-bin and radial bound for the inside case (single edge).

Fills intThetas/intWeights with Gauss nodes/weights on [θ_start, θ_end], and sets r_max(θ) = d0 / cos(θ − θ0), r_min = 0.

Definition at line 185 of file TriQuadPol.cpp.

Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ adaptOut()

void TriQuadPol::adaptOut ( double theta0,
double thetaStart,
double thetaEnd,
double tLineFirst,
double dLineFirst,
double tLineSecond,
double dLineSecond,
std::vector< double > & intThetas,
std::vector< double > & intWeights,
std::vector< double > & minRadius,
std::vector< double > & maxRadius )

Build angular bins/radial bounds when the point is outside the triangle.

Uses two boundary lines per angular sector (enter/exit).

Parameters
theta0Global rotation added to all output angles.
thetaStart,thetaEndSector angular limits.
tLineFirst,dLineFirstFirst line parameters \(\{\theta_0, d_0\}\).
tLineSecond,dLineSecondSecond line parameters \(\{\theta_0, d_0\}\).
intThetas,intWeightsOutput Gauss nodes/weights in \(\theta\).
minRadius,maxRadiusOutput inner/outer radii for each \(\theta\) bin.

Build a θ-bin and two radial bounds for the outside case (two edges).

Sets r_min/max(θ) from two edge lines; swaps if min > max numerically.

Definition at line 212 of file TriQuadPol.cpp.

Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ adaptPolar()

void TriQuadPol::adaptPolar ( )

Build angular sectors and radial bounds, then assemble nodes/weights.

Calls, in order: transformInPlane() -> adaptIn()/adaptOut() -> assembleFromPolar(). Safe to call multiple times (clears previous results).

Main driver: project to plane, split angular sectors, and assemble nodes/weights.

Clears previous results, then: 1) transformInPlane() 2) Decide inside/outside and compute sector data via adaptIn()/adaptOut() 3) assembleFromPolar()

Definition at line 24 of file TriQuadPol.cpp.

◆ assembleFromPolar()

void TriQuadPol::assembleFromPolar ( const std::vector< double > & inThetas,
const std::vector< double > & inWeights,
const std::vector< double > & inMinRadii,
const std::vector< double > & inMaxRadii )

Expand \(\theta\)-bins into ( \(\theta, r\)) Gauss products and convert to barycentric nodes.

Parameters
inThetasVector of \(\theta\) nodes (bin centers).
inWeightsVector of \(\theta\) weights.
inMinRadiiVector of \(r_{min}\) per \(\theta\) node.
inMaxRadiiVector of \(r_{max}\) per \(\theta\) node.

Expand (θ,r) Gauss products and convert to barycentric coordinates.

Each θ-bin is expanded by nQuadrature radial nodes. The Jacobian factor r and the radial span (r_max - r_min) are included in the weights.

◆ lineInPolar()

std::pair< double, double > TriQuadPol::lineInPolar ( double theta1,
double rho1,
double theta2,
double rho2 )

Straight-line segment between two vertices written in polar form about posInPlane.

For a line through \((\theta_1,\rho_1)\) and \((\theta_2,\rho_2)\) in polar coords around posInPlane, returns \((\theta_0,\rho_0)\) such that \( r(\theta) = d_0 / cos(\theta − \theta_0)\) on that segment.

Parameters
theta1,rho1Endpoint 1 in polar coords.
theta2,rho2Endpoint 2 in polar coords.
Returns
Pair \(\{\theta_0, d_0\}\).

Return line parameters (θ0,d0) for the edge between two vertices in polar coordinates.

To avoid degeneracy when θ1 == θ2, a tiny perturbation is applied.

Definition at line 167 of file TriQuadPol.cpp.

◆ transformInPlane()

void TriQuadPol::transformInPlane ( )

Build an orthonormal basis on the triangle plane and project (vertices, point).

Sets vertsInPlane and posInPlane in a local (e1,e2) frame with vertex 0 at (0,0).

Builds an orthonormal basis (e1 along edge 0->1, e2 = e3 x e1, e3 normal), then expresses vertices and the observation point in that basis.
